##NAMES=Tridymite
##RRUFFID=R090042
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=SiO_2_
##LOCALITY=The Fukang Pallasite meteorite, Gobi Desert, China, found in 2001
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Dante Lauretta
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless micro-crystal, as an inclusion in olivine. Masami Kanzaki (Okayama University) suggests this is polytype tridymite MC.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R090042
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 25.938 b: 5.015 c: 18.547 alpha: 90 beta: 117.68 gamma: 90 volume: 2136 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
